english_name: The Revelation of Saint John the Divine

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vidi alium angelum fortem descendentem de cælo amictum nube, et iris in capite ejus, et facies ejus erat ut sol, et pedes ejus tamquam columnæ ignis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶδον ἄλλον ἄγγελον ἰσχυρὸν καταβαίνοντα ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ περιβεβλημένον νεφέλην καὶ ἶρις ἐπὶ τῆς κεφαλῆς καὶ τὸ πρόσωπον αὐτοῦ ὡς ὁ ἥλιος καὶ οἱ πόδες αὐτοῦ ὡς στῦλοι πυρός
verses.king_james: And I saw another mighty angel come down from heaven, clothed with a cloud: and a rainbow *was* upon his head, and his face *was* as it were the sun, and his feet as pillars of fire:

verses.latin_vulgate: et habebat in manu sua libellum apertum: et posuit pedem suum dextrum super mare, sinistrum autem super terram:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἴχεν ἐν τῇ χειρὶ αὐτοῦ βιβλαρίδιον ἀνεῳγμένον καὶ ἔθηκεν τὸν πόδα αὐτοῦ τὸν δεξιὸν ἐπὶ τὴν θάλασσαν τὸν δὲ εὐώνυμον ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν
verses.king_james: And he had in his hand a little book open: and he set his right foot upon the sea, and *his* left *foot* on the earth,

verses.latin_vulgate: et clamavit voce magna, quemadmodum cum leo rugit. Et cum clamasset, locuta sunt septem tonitrua voces suas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔκραξεν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ὥσπερ λέων μυκᾶται καὶ ὅτε ἔκραξεν ἐλάλησαν αἱ ἑπτὰ βρονταὶ τὰς ἑαυτῶν φωνάς
verses.king_james: And cried with a loud voice, as *when* a lion roareth: and when he had cried, seven thunders uttered their voices.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum locuta fuissent septem tonitrua voces suas, ego scripturus eram: et audivi vocem de cælo dicentem mihi: Signa quæ locuta sunt septem tonitrua: et noli ea scribere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε ἐλάλησαν αἱ ἑπτὰ βρονταί τὰς φωνὰς ἑαυτῶν ἔμελλον γράφειν καὶ ἤκουσα φωνὴν ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ λέγουσαν μοι σφράγισον ἃ ἐλάλησαν αἱ ἑπτὰ βρονταί καὶ μὴ ταὐτὰ γράψῃς
verses.king_james: And when the seven thunders had uttered their voices, I was about to write: and I heard a voice from heaven saying unto me, Seal up those things which the seven thunders uttered, and write them not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et angelus, quem vidi stantem super mare et super terram, levavit manum suam ad cælum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ ἄγγελος ὃν εἶδον ἑστῶτα ἐπὶ τῆς θαλάσσης καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἦρεν τὴν χεῖρα αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν
verses.king_james: And the angel which I saw stand upon the sea and upon the earth lifted up his hand to heaven,

verses.latin_vulgate: et juravit per viventem in sæcula sæculorum, qui creavit cælum, et ea quæ in eo sunt: et terram, et ea quæ in ea sunt: et mare, et ea quæ in eo sunt: Quia tempus non erit amplius:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὤμοσεν ἐν τῷ ζῶντι εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων ὃς ἔκτισεν τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὰ ἐν αὐτῷ καὶ τὴν γῆν καὶ τὰ ἐν αὐτῇ καὶ τὴν θάλασσαν καὶ τὰ ἐν αὐτῇ ὅτι χρόνος οὐκ ἔσται έτι
verses.king_james: And sware by him that liveth for ever and ever, who created heaven, and the things that therein are, and the earth, and the things that therein are, and the sea, and the things which are therein, that there should be time no longer:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed in diebus vocis septimi angeli, cum cœperit tuba canere, consummabitur mysterium Dei sicut evangelizavit per servos suos prophetas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις τῆς φωνῆς τοῦ ἑβδόμου ἀγγέλου ὅταν μέλλῃ σαλπίζειν καὶ τελεσθῇ τὸ μυστήριον τοῦ θεοῦ ὡς εὐηγγέλισεν τοῖς ἑαυτοῦ δούλοις τοῖς προφήταις
verses.king_james: But in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he shall begin to sound, the mystery of God should be finished, as he hath declared to his servants the prophets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et audivi vocem de cælo iterum loquentem mecum, et dicentem: Vade, et accipe librum apertum de manu angeli stantis super mare, et super terram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἡ φωνὴ ἣν ἤκουσα ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ πάλιν λαλοῦσα μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ καὶ λέγουσα Ὕπαγε λάβε τὸ βιβλαρίδιον τὸ ἠνεῳγμένον ἐν τῇ χειρὶ ἀγγέλου τοῦ ἑστῶτος ἐπὶ τῆς θαλάσσης καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: And the voice which I heard from heaven spake unto me again, and said, Go *and* ake the little book which is open in the hand of the angel which standeth upon the sea and upon the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et abii ad angelum, dicens ei, ut daret mihi librum. Et dixit mihi: Accipe librum, et devora illum: et faciet amaricari ventrem tuum, sed in ore tuo erit dulce tamquam mel.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπῆλθον πρὸς τὸν ἄγγελον λέγων αὐτῷ δός μοι τὸ βιβλαρίδιον καὶ λέγει μοι λάβε καὶ κατάφαγε αὐτό καὶ πικρανεῖ σου τὴν κοιλίαν ἀλλ᾽ ἐν τῷ στόματί σου ἔσται γλυκὺ ὡς μέλι
verses.king_james: And I went unto the angel, and said unto him, Give me the little book. And he said unto me, Take *it*, and eat it up; and it shall make thy belly bitter, but it shall be in thy mouth sweet as honey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accepi librum de manu angeli, et devoravi illum: et erat in ore meo tamquam mel dulce, et cum devorassem eum, amaricatus est venter meus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλαβον τὸ βιβλαρίδιον ἐκ τῆς χειρὸς τοῦ ἀγγέλου καὶ κατέφαγον αὐτό καὶ ἦν ἐν τῷ στόματί μου ὡς μέλι γλυκύ καὶ ὅτε ἔφαγον αὐτό ἐπικράνθη ἡ κοιλία μου
verses.king_james: And I took the little book out of the angel's hand, and ate it up; and it was in my mouth sweet as honey: and as soon as I had eaten it, my belly was bitter.

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit mihi: Oportet te iterum prophetare gentibus, et populis, et linguis, et regibus multis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει μοι δεῖ σε πάλιν προφητεῦσαι ἐπὶ λαοῖς καὶ ἔθνεσιν καὶ γλώσσαις καὶ βασιλεῦσιν πολλοῖς
verses.king_james: And he said unto me, Thou must prophesy again before many peoples, and nations, and tongues, and kings.
